metrics.title: FY26 EPS Guide
metrics.label: Trough cycle; expected to recover from FY25

metrics.title: Operating Margin
metrics.label: Down from ~22% peak; recovering on cycle

moat.description: Dominant North American farm equipment franchise with the largest agricultural dealer network and a credible precision-ag software franchise — durable but cyclical, with farmer balance sheets currently soft.
moat.analysisSummary: Deere's moat is **the largest agricultural dealer network in the West paired with a growing precision-ag software franchise** — durable on hardware, with optionality on software:

analysisPoints.title: Dealer Network Density
analysisPoints.text: Deere's North American dealer footprint is unmatched — closer to farms, faster service, and parts availability that competitors (CNH, AGCO, Kubota) cannot match in core US/Canadian markets. Switching tractor brands means losing service proximity, which is a first-order farmer concern in season.

analysisPoints.title: Precision Ag Software Optionality
analysisPoints.text: John Deere Operations Centre + See & Spray + autonomous tractors are real software franchises with ~$1B ARR run-rate growing 30%+. The recurring-revenue layer compounds on the installed base of ~750K connected machines and is a meaningful moat extension if execution holds.

analysisPoints.title: Cyclical Discipline
analysisPoints.text: Through-cycle margin discipline (operating margin ~14-22%) and capital return have been industry-leading. Deere's mid-cycle through-cycle margin floor of ~16% holds even in farmer-soft cycles like FY25-26 — better than CNH/AGCO.

growth.description: FY26 EPS guide $22-25, recovering modestly from FY25 trough. Farmer balance sheets remain soft on grain prices; the cyclical recovery is back-end-loaded into FY27. Long-term thesis is precision ag software ARR + autonomous farming TAM expansion against cyclical hardware base.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 65 (4-8% CAGR top of band) + 5 precision ag ARR optionality (30%+ growth) - 5 cyclicality (FY25-26 trough) - 5 farmer balance sheet (grain prices weak) = 60

drivers.name: Agriculture & Turf
drivers.metric: Trough cycle FY25-26; recovery dependent on grain prices and farmer income

drivers.name: Construction & Forestry
drivers.metric: Tied to non-residential construction cycle; mixed

drivers.name: Precision Ag Software ARR
drivers.metric: $1B run-rate growing 30%+; subscription mix shift in progress

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If farmer income remains depressed through 2026-27 due to sustained low grain prices and high input costs, the equipment replacement cycle delays further and Deere's earnings power compresses to $20 EPS, triggering a multiple compression to 13-14×.

valuation.description: At ~$485 Deere trades at ~21× trough FY26 EPS — premium reflecting precision-ag software optionality, but the cyclical pattern argues for waiting for through-cycle multiple. Through-cycle valuation is more like 14-16× normalised earnings.

peAnalysis.summary: Valuation prices in cyclical recovery and software optionality. Margin of safety is modest at current levels.

bear.description: Farmer income stays soft through 2027, equipment cycle bottom delays, multiple compresses to 14× depressed earnings.
points: Grain prices remain low through 2027; farmer income stays below long-term average
Equipment replacement cycle delays into 2028, deferring revenue recovery
Precision ag software ARR growth slows below 20% on slower hardware install base

base.description: Cyclical recovery in FY27 drives EPS to $30, precision ag ARR continues compounding, multiple sustains 18×.
points: Grain prices recover modestly in 2026-27, farmer income normalises
FY27 EPS reaches $30 on volume + price + cost discipline
Precision ag ARR exceeds $1.4B run-rate by FY27

bull.description: Cyclical recovery + autonomous tractor commercial launch drives premium multiple expansion to 22×, FY28 EPS reaches $35+.
points: Autonomous tractor commercial deployment accelerates in 2026-27
Precision ag ARR surpasses $2B by FY28 with 90%+ gross margins
Capital return increases as cycle EBITDA recovers above 25%

learnedInterfaces.note: Farmers and operators learn Deere controls and Operations Centre over years; some switching friction at the operator level.

businessLogic.note: Operations Centre + See & Spray + autonomous tractor software encode farm-level business logic that compounds with installed base.

talentScarcity.note: Agricultural-engineering and dealer-service technician bench is real and durable — Deere's training pipeline is decades old.

bundling.note: Tractor + implements + precision ag software + financing bundle is the most complete in the industry; rivals address subsets.

proprietaryData.note: Operations Centre data on ~750K connected machines covering ~325M acres globally is genuinely unique — feeds See & Spray, autonomy, and agronomic insights.

regulatoryLockIn.note: EPA emissions, NHTSA on-road, and global homologation create real new-entrant barriers in agricultural equipment.

networkEffects.note: Dealer network density creates a flywheel — more Deere machines in a region attract more dealer investment, attracting more buyers.

transactionEmbedding.note: John Deere Financial finances ~50% of equipment, dealer parts contracts, and precision-ag subscriptions create deep recurring embedment.

systemOfRecord.note: Operations Centre is becoming the system of record for many farm operations — agronomy, equipment, financials in one platform.

tenMoats.verdict: Deere is a high-quality industrial-AI franchise — autonomous farming and computer vision (See & Spray) compound the moat. The thesis question is cyclical (farmer balance sheet) not technological. AI is structurally additive.
